items.claim: Conor McGregor historically struggles to overcome adversity in fights.
items.quote: even in Conor McGregor's prime, he wasn't known for overcoming adversity.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's performance significantly declines as fights go deep.
items.quote: He wasn't known for doing well deep into fights.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is prone to an adrenaline dump that causes his cardio to fail early in fights.
items.quote: there's going to be this adrenaline dump where all of a sudden Connor starts huffing and puffing.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is coming off a 5-year layoff and a leg injury.
items.quote: what do you think it's going to be like off a 5-year layoff with a leg injury?

items.claim: Max Holloway's striking volume will overwhelm Conor McGregor if the fight extends past the first three minutes.
items.quote: if he cannot put Max down in those first three minutes, Max is going to punch him in the face over and over and over and over and over again.

items.claim: Patty Pimblett has poor striking defense.
items.quote: he's definitely gotten away with a lot to make it to a title shot with that kind of strike defense

items.claim: Patty Pimblett has physically increased his size for the lightweight division.
items.quote: I also noticed the size he's put on for the weight class.

items.claim: Fighters who lose a title fight often experience a decline in performance and go on losing skids.
items.quote: when you lose out on that title shot and you realize, hey, I'm not good enough to be champion, not always, but a lot of the time fighters tend to to drop a few more fights and go on skids
